persistent_storage.rationale: Binary columnar eliminates text parsing (10min -> <1s for 6M rows) and enables column pruning (Q1 reads 8/16 lineitem columns = 50% I/O savings). Essential for repeated query execution.

indexes.name: lineitem_shipdate_zonemap
indexes.rationale: All 3 queries filter on l_shipdate. Sorting by l_shipdate enables near-perfect block skipping (Q1: 75% blocks skipped, Q6: 86% skipped, Q3: 60% skipped).

indexes.name: lineitem_orderkey_hash
indexes.rationale: Q3 joins lineitem with orders on l_orderkey. Hash index enables O(1) probe lookups during hash join.

indexes.name: lineitem_returnflag_dict
indexes.rationale: Dictionary encoding: 3 unique values (A, N, R). Reduces 1-byte CHAR to <1 bit, 87% compression. Enables integer comparisons in GROUP BY.

indexes.name: lineitem_linestatus_dict
indexes.rationale: Dictionary encoding: 2 unique values (O, F). Reduces 1-byte CHAR to 1 bit, 87% compression. Enables integer comparisons in GROUP BY.

column_groups.name: arithmetic_group
column_groups.rationale: Q1 and Q3 compute expressions involving all three columns. Storing adjacently improves cache locality during vectorized arithmetic.

column_groups.name: group_key_group
column_groups.rationale: Q1 groups by both columns. Adjacent storage enables fused composite key hashing.

indexes.name: orders_orderkey_sorted
indexes.rationale: Primary key. Sorted index enables binary search for point lookups and sorted merge join with lineitem.

indexes.name: orders_custkey_hash
indexes.rationale: Q3 joins orders with customer on o_custkey. Hash index enables O(1) probe during hash join.

indexes.name: orders_orderdate_zonemap
indexes.rationale: Q3 filters o_orderdate < '1995-03-15'. TPC-H data is temporally ordered, enabling 40% block skipping.

indexes.name: customer_custkey_sorted
indexes.rationale: Primary key. Small dimension table (150K rows), full scan with filter acceptable.

indexes.name: customer_mktsegment_dict
indexes.rationale: Dictionary encoding: 5 unique values (market segments). Q3 filters by single segment (20% selectivity).

indexes.name: part_partkey_sorted
indexes.rationale: Primary key. Not accessed in current workload, minimal indexing needed.

indexes.name: partsupp_composite_sorted
indexes.rationale: Composite primary key. Not accessed in current workload.

indexes.name: supplier_suppkey_sorted
indexes.rationale: Primary key. Not accessed in current workload.

indexes.name: nation_nationkey_sorted
indexes.rationale: Primary key. Tiny dimension (25 rows), no special indexing needed.

indexes.name: region_regionkey_sorted
indexes.rationale: Primary key. Tiny dimension (5 rows), no special indexing needed.

Q1.query_description: Scan lineitem, filter by l_shipdate, aggregate by (l_returnflag, l_linestatus)

index_usage.rationale: Date filter l_shipdate <= '1998-09-02'. Zone maps skip blocks with min > '1998-09-02', eliminating ~25% of blocks (3 months out of 7 years).

predicate_pushdown.rationale: Evaluate date filter during scan before materializing tuples. Reduces data volume by 25% before aggregation.

Q1.estimated_io_bytes: ~168MB (6M rows * 7 columns * avg 4 bytes, accounting for dictionaries)

Q3.query_description: Join customer-orders-lineitem, filter by dates and c_mktsegment, aggregate by (l_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority), top-10 by revenue

index_usage.rationale: Dictionary-encoded c_mktsegment filter (1 of 5 segments). Integer comparison on encoded values, 80% reduction.
Hash join customer-orders on custkey. Build hash table on filtered customer (~30K rows), probe with orders.
Date filter o_orderdate < '1995-03-15'. Zone maps skip blocks with min >= '1995-03-15', eliminating 40% of blocks.
Hash join orders-lineitem on orderkey. Build hash table on filtered orders (~600K rows), probe with lineitem.
Date filter l_shipdate > '1995-03-15'. Zone maps skip blocks with max <= '1995-03-15', eliminating 40% of blocks.

predicate_pushdown.rationale: Filter customer table during scan. Reduces build side of customer-orders join from 150K to 30K rows (5x reduction).
Filter orders table during scan. Reduces probe side of customer-orders join and build side of orders-lineitem join by 40%.
Filter lineitem table during scan. Reduces probe side of orders-lineitem join by 40%.

join_ordering.rationale: Build on smallest filtered table. Customer filter reduces to 30K rows, minimizing hash table size.

join_ordering.build: orders (filtered to 600K rows after join and date filter)
join_ordering.rationale: Build on intermediate result. Orders-customer join + date filter yields ~600K rows, much smaller than lineitem.

Q6.query_description: Scan lineitem, filter by l_shipdate range, l_discount range, l_quantity, aggregate SUM(revenue)

index_usage.rationale: Date range filter l_shipdate BETWEEN '1994-01-01' AND '1994-12-31'. Zone maps skip all blocks outside 1994 window, eliminating 86% of blocks (1 year out of 7 years).

predicate_pushdown.rationale: Date range filter during scan. Zone maps enable highly selective block skipping (86% of data eliminated).
Narrow discount range filter during scan. Evaluate together with date filter in fused SIMD loop for 90% combined selectivity.
Quantity filter during scan. Final predicate in fused filter chain, combined selectivity ~99.3% (only ~42K rows pass all filters).

Q6.estimated_io_bytes: ~96MB (6M rows * 4 columns * avg 4 bytes, but zone maps reduce to 14% of blocks = ~13MB effective I/O)

lineitem.rationale: Primary sort by l_shipdate for zone map effectiveness (all queries filter on dates). Secondary sort by l_orderkey maintains TPC-H clustering and helps Q3 join.

orders.rationale: Primary key sort. TPC-H data already ordered by o_orderkey, minimal sorting cost.

lineitem.rationale: Zone maps on l_shipdate critical for all queries. Block size = 64K rows ~= L3_cache / num_threads (44MB / 64 threads / 16 cols ~= 40KB per morsel).

orders.rationale: Zone maps on o_orderdate for Q3 filtering. Same block size for consistent morsel-driven parallelism.

ingestion.estimated_ingestion_time: ~60 seconds for SF=1 (6M rows lineitem + sorting + index building)
ingestion.rationale: Parallel ingestion across 64 threads: parse text, sort lineitem by l_shipdate, build dictionaries, construct zone maps, write binary column files. One-time cost amortized across many query executions.

type_mappings.notes: Dates encoded as int32_t (days since epoch 1970-01-01) for fast integer comparison. DECIMAL mapped to double for simplicity (exact decimal not required for TPC-H aggregations).

hardware_config.morsel_size_calculation: L3_cache / num_threads / num_columns = 44MB / 64 / 16 ≈ 40KB ≈ 10K-16K rows per morsel

expected_memory_footprint_per_query.Q3: ~500MB (10 columns across 3 tables, join intermediates, hash tables)
expected_memory_footprint_per_query.Q6: ~50MB (4 columns * 6M rows, highly filtered by zone maps)

design_rationale.storage_format: Binary columnar storage eliminates text parsing (6M rows: CSV parsing ~10min, mmap <1s) and enables column pruning (Q1 reads 50% fewer bytes by skipping unused columns). Essential for repeated query execution.
design_rationale.sort_keys: Sort lineitem by l_shipdate (composite: l_shipdate, l_orderkey). All 3 queries filter on l_shipdate with high selectivity. Sorting enables zone map block skipping: Q1 skips 25%, Q3 skips 40%, Q6 skips 86% of blocks. Secondary sort by l_orderkey preserves TPC-H clustering for Q3 join.
design_rationale.block_size: 64K rows per block balances zone map granularity (finer = better skipping) with metadata overhead. At 64K rows/block, lineitem has ~92 blocks, each ~2.5MB. Fits L3 cache budget (44MB / 64 threads = 688KB per thread).
design_rationale.compression: Dictionary encoding for low-cardinality columns (l_returnflag: 3 values, l_linestatus: 2 values, c_mktsegment: 5 values) reduces memory footprint by 80-90% and enables integer comparisons during aggregation. High-entropy columns (extendedprice, quantities) left uncompressed to avoid decompression overhead.
design_rationale.indexes: Zone maps on date columns (l_shipdate, o_orderdate) provide 10-100x speedup via block skipping on HDD. Hash indexes on join keys (l_orderkey, o_custkey) enable O(1) hash join probes. Sorted indexes on primary keys (o_orderkey, c_custkey) support binary search and merge joins.
design_rationale.io_strategy: Per-query column pruning: Q1 reads 7/16 lineitem columns (56% savings), Q6 reads 4/16 (75% savings), Q3 reads 10/33 total columns across 3 tables (70% savings). Predicate pushdown evaluates filters during scan before materialization. Zone maps eliminate 25-86% of I/O for date-filtered queries.
design_rationale.parallelism: 64-thread morsel-driven parallelism on lineitem scans (6M rows / 64 threads = 93K rows/thread, ~6 morsels/thread at 16K morsel size). Partitioned hash joins for Q3 (partition both sides by join key, join partitions independently). Expected speedup: 50-60x vs single-threaded (slightly sub-linear due to merge overhead).
design_rationale.hdd_optimization: HDD sequential read: ~150MB/s, random read: ~1MB/s. Zone maps critical on HDD to avoid random I/O. Sorting by l_shipdate transforms date range queries into sequential scans over contiguous blocks. Q6 effective I/O: 13MB sequential (86% blocks skipped) vs 96MB full scan.

summary: This design optimizes for execution time on a TPC-H workload dominated by date-filtered scans and joins. Binary columnar storage eliminates text parsing (10min -> <1s). Sorting lineitem by l_shipdate enables zone map block skipping (25-86% blocks skipped across queries). Dictionary encoding compresses low-cardinality columns by 80-90%. Hash indexes on join keys (l_orderkey, o_custkey) enable O(1) hash join probes. Per-query column pruning saves 56-75% I/O. Morsel-driven parallelism across 64 threads with 16K morsel size maximizes CPU utilization (expected 50-60x speedup). HDD-optimized: zone maps avoid random I/O, sequential scans leverage 150MB/s bandwidth. Expected query times: Q1 ~300ms, Q3 ~1.5s, Q6 ~150ms (vs 3-4s, 15-20s, 2s single-threaded baseline).
